channel.title: Inference — AI Newsletter
channel.description: The signal in the noise. Curated AI trends, tools, and research — updated every Monday.

item.title: Trump Moves to Ban Anthropic From the US Government
item.description: Depending on your setup, Trump might save you time or headaches down the line (via Wired AI).
item.author: Will Knight

item.title: Introducing the Stateful Runtime Environment for Agents in Amazon Bedrock
item.description: A fresh release of Stateful Runtime Environment is now available for teams ready to evaluate alternatives (via OpenAI Blog).

item.title: Musk bashes OpenAI in deposition, saying ‘nobody committed suicide because of Grok’
item.description: If Musk pans out, it could change how teams approach their current stack (via TechCrunch AI).
item.author: Sarah Perez

item.title: Pentagon moves to designate Anthropic as a supply-chain risk
item.description: The president wrote in the post.
item.author: Russell Brandom

item.title: Anthropic Hits Back After US Military Labels It a ‘Supply Chain Risk’
item.description: Not a fire drill, but Anthropic is the kind of thing that compounds if you ignore it (via Wired AI).
item.author: Maxwell Zeff, Will Knight, Lauren Goode et al.

item.title: AI vs. the Pentagon: killer robots, mass surveillance, and red lines
item.description: The Pentagon story has enough signal to track — file it for your next planning round (via The Verge AI).
item.author: Stevie Bonifield

item.title: Perplexity announces "Computer," an AI agent that assigns work to other AI agents
item.description: Personnel shifts at Perplexity can foreshadow product or API changes — factor into vendor review (via Ars Technica AI).
item.author: Samuel Axon

item.title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
item.description: Security developments around Case may require patching or posture changes for affected teams (via Dark Reading).
item.author: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Finding value with AI and Industry 5.0 transformation
item.description: The Finding story has enough signal to track — file it for your next planning round (via MIT Technology Review AI).
item.author: MIT Technology Review Insights

item.title: A Coding Implementation to Build a Hierarchical Planner AI Agent Using Open-Source LLMs with Tool Execution and Structured Multi-Agent Reasoning
item.description: The Coding Implementation paper offers techniques that applied teams should track for downstream impact (via MarkTechPost).
item.author: Michal Sutter

item.title: Deploying Open Source Vision Language Models (VLM) on Jetson
item.description: Open access to VLM gives teams a new self-hosted option to evaluate (via Hugging Face Blog).

item.title: Agent Economy Daily #11: NIST Makes It Official — AI Agents Get Their Own Standards Body
item.description: Teams already working in this space should skim the Agent Economy Daily details (via Dev.to AI).
item.author: Neil Volner

item.title: Get more context and understand translations more deeply with new AI-powered updates in Translate.
item.description: Not a fire drill, but Translate is the kind of thing that compounds if you ignore it (via Google AI Blog).
item.author: Matt Sheets

item.title: The human work behind humanoid robots is being hidden
item.description: Not a fire drill, but The human work behind humanoid is the kind of thing that compounds if you ignore it (via MIT Technology Review AI).
item.author: James O'Donnell

item.title: Deep-Dive into LLM Fine-Tuning
item.description: Model updates around Dive may change which option fits your workload and budget (via Hacker News).

item.title: AI’s Math Tricks Don’t Work for Scientific Computing
item.description: The Math Tricks Don story has enough signal to track — file it for your next planning round (via IEEE Spectrum AI).
item.author: Dina Genkina
